page: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g-11520s is a member of the VX1 Cloud Compute server family, featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by AMD processors. The configuration provides 192 vCPUs and 192 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server includes a substantial local storage capacity of 11520 GB on NVMe SSDs, which is bundled directly with the instance. It also includes 1 complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no integrated GPUs present on this instance type.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a high-capacity general-purpose profile. The combination of a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and massive local NVMe storage makes it suitable for large-scale data processing, distributed databases, and general-purpose workloads requiring significant local storage capacity. However, the shared CPU allocation model is a key tradeoff to consider for workloads that require sustained, isolated CPU performance.
description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g-11520s is an x86_64 general-purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 192 AMD vCPUs and 192 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is a key feature of this configuration, consisting of 11520 GB of bundled NVMe SSD space. The instance does not include any GPUs but does provide 1 complimentary public IPv4 address. Given its high resource density and balanced memory-to-core ratio, this server is designed for large-scale general-purpose applications, distributed databases, and data-intensive workloads that benefit from massive local NVMe storage, though the shared CPU allocation represents a performance tradeoff for highly sustained compute tasks.
